name: Muslim American Youth Foundation
mission: Our mission is to educate and instil a drive for personal academic success, enrich through experience and expansion of what is really possible, and enlighten by means of taking the aforementioned experiences and incorporating them into their personal lives, households and communities.
theoryOfChange: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) outlines its strategic approach under the 'Our Strategy' section on its 'About Us' page and further elaborates on its programs. This framework details how their programs in learning, athletics, and fostering success are designed to help youth develop a strong sense of personal, academic, and spiritual identity, and equip them with skills and knowledge to overcome challenges. Their purpose is further described through initiatives in outreach, advocacy, education, youth empowerment, resource dissemination, and community service, all aimed at fostering personal growth and providing holistic support to Muslim-American youth.
scoreSummary: Muslim American Youth Foundation shows strong impact and good alignment.

evaluationSources: Healthy Environment for All (HEAL) Capacity Grant (Washington State Department of Health (DOH))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

score_details.score_summary: Muslim American Youth Foundation shows strong impact and good alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: The Muslim American Youth Foundation's donation page and mission statement do not explicitly mention 'zakat' or dedicated zakat funds. While an affiliated organization, the Islamic Association of North America (IANA), has a 'Zakat Calculation' link, the prompt specifies that international affiliates or parent organizations are different entities and evidence must be from the specific US organization being evaluated. (Source: https://www.mayf.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: The organization holds 13.4 months of working capital and focuses on youth education and enrichment.
The foundation reported total revenue of $2,087,958 and a program expense ratio of 72.1%.
The organization operates programs for community services, youth sports, athletics, and mentoring.
The organization accepts Sadaqah-eligible donations through its website.
The foundation offers volunteer opportunities to engage the community in its youth development mission.

Limited third-party impact evaluations available for specific program outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, MAYF offers a medium fit for Muslim donors looking to support domestic poverty alleviation and youth enrichment [4]. The organization provides various engagement pathways, including volunteer opportunities and direct donation options to support its community-centric mission [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates a high capacity for growth and stability, maintaining 13.4 months of working capital to ensure program continuity [1][2]. With a program expense ratio of 72.1%, the majority of funds are directed toward community services, youth sports, and mentoring initiatives designed to build local capacity [2][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Youth Foundation provides a dedicated environment for youth education, enrichment, and athletic mentoring to foster community development.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.02 spent per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 13.4 months of working capital
Direct focus on domestic youth development and mentoring
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) operates as a non-profit organization dedicated to creating facilities for youth enlightenment and community services [1][3]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $2,087,958 to support its mission of providing educational and athletic programs [2].

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="2">72.1% program expense ratio</cite>, which indicates that 40% of spending is directed toward non-programmatic costs. Additionally, there is <cite id="1">no publicly available cost-per-beneficiary data</cite>.

confidence.notes: Financial data is strong but impact data is limited.

alignment.explanation: The organization is a strong fit for donors interested in <cite id="3">domestic youth development</cite> and Islamic community infrastructure. It serves <cite id="1">underserved populations</cite> in the USA through faith-based and athletic programs.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in the organization's <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and its <cite id="2">detailed annual tax filings</cite> which show clear asset management. The charity also maintains a <cite id="1">high working capital ratio</cite>, suggesting it is not at risk of short-term financial distress.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be enhanced by undergoing a full third-party audit and <cite id="1">improving transparency scores</cite> through platforms like Candid.

impact.explanation: The foundation delivers impact through a <cite id="3">capacity-building model</cite>, providing physical facilities for youth sports and health advocacy. It currently maintains a <cite id="2">72.1% program expense ratio</cite> while scaling its reach.
impact.improvement: To improve impact scores, the charity should <cite id="1">document and publish specific triggers</cite> for when its high reserves will be deployed into direct youth services.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while a theory of change is documented, <cite id="1">beneficiary counts and cost-effectiveness metrics</cite> are not yet public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation uses physical facilities to provide a structured environment for <cite id="3">mentoring, sports, and health advocacy</cite>, leading to improved youth outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the $1.5 million in annual contributions is effectively translating into improved life outcomes for the youth served.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The organization is in a rapid growth phase with a <cite id="2">73.3% CAGR</cite>, requiring capital for facility expansion.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="1">exceptionally low fundraising cost</cite> and a very high <cite id="1">27.7-month reserve cushion</cite>.

strengths.detail: The charity spends only <cite id="1">$0.03 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, significantly outperforming sector averages for efficiency.
With <cite id="1">13.4 months of working capital</cite> held in working capital, the organization has a high level of financial resilience.
The foundation has demonstrated a <cite id="2">73.3% three-year revenue growth rate</cite>, indicating strong community support and scaling capacity.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim American Youth Foundation demonstrates a lean operational model regarding its resource acquisition. By maintaining a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>, the organization ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are available for its core mission. This fiscal discipline is paired with a <cite id="1">100% independent board of directors</cite>, which provides critical oversight for its <cite id="2">$5.3 million in total assets</cite>. Furthermore, the organization has successfully diversified its income, generating <cite id="2">$225,128 from program service revenue</cite> alongside its primary contribution base.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) operates as a domestic hub for youth development, focusing on education, enrichment, and athletic mentoring. Based in the United States, the organization reported <cite id="2">$1,734,105 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. This represents a period of significant expansion, as the charity has achieved a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 73.3%</cite>. MAYF manages a diverse portfolio of community services, including <cite id="3">youth sports, health advocacy, and faith-based programs</cite>. The organization maintains a strong financial position with <cite id="1">13.4 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, ensuring operational stability for its facility-based services. While the charity is deeply rooted in the Muslim community, it is currently designated as <cite id="4">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite>, meaning it provides general humanitarian and community support rather than specialized zakat-restricted aid. The foundation's model emphasizes capacity building, using its facilities to create a <cite id="3">safe environment for youth enlightenment</cite> and community engagement.
